clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guidance on moderate potassium supplementation for BP management in adults with elevated BP or hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with elevated BP or hypertension, excluding CKD or potassium-retaining drug use
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend moderate potassium supplementation (<80 mmol/d) with monitoring conditions

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for potassium supplementation assessment
Confirm elevated BP or hypertension diagnosis
Screen for CKD or potassium-retaining medications
Select dietary vs supplemental potassium source
Set serum potassium monitoring if indicated

validationRules: Either hasElevatedBP OR hasHypertension must be true
If hasCKD true OR onPotassiumRetainingDrugs true → require monitoring

rules: Check if patient has elevated BP or hypertension
If CKD present OR on potassium-retaining drugs → flag monitoring requirement
Prefer dietary potassium sources over supplements
If neither CKD nor potassium-retaining drugs → standard recommendation without mandatory monitoring
Apply COR 1 (STRONG) + LOE A (High-quality evidence from RCTs)

categories.name: No Monitoring Required
categories.condition: No CKD AND no potassium-retaining drugs

loeDescription.description: High-quality evidence from more than 1 RCT, meta-analyses of high-quality RCTs, or RCTs corroborated by high-quality registry studies

recommendation.description: In adults with elevated BP or hypertension, moderate potassium supplementation, ideally from dietary sources, is recommended to prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension, except in the presence of CKD or use of drugs that reduce potassium excretion where monitoring of serum potassium levels is indicated

requiresMonitoring.description: true if CKD or potassium-retaining drugs present

symptomsToAsk: Signs of hyperkalemia (muscle weakness, fatigue, palpitations) if on supplementation

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Check serum potassium immediately, adjust supplementation
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Continue with monitoring per CKD/drug status

appDisplay.screenType: Standard recommendation display with COR/LOE badge
appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing with monitoring alerts if applicable

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Initiate moderate potassium supplementation (<80 mmol/d) from dietary sources
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Check serum potassium at baseline and periodically if CKD or on potassium-retaining drugs
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Alert if potassium dose exceeds 80 mmol/d or if serum potassium abnormal
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log COR 1 | LOE A recommendation to health record with monitoring plan
